title: The Fog on the Glass
text_zh: 冬天的咖啡馆里，靠窗的座位总是最先被占满。不是因为风景好——外面灰蒙蒙的，树也秃了——而是因为玻璃上会起一层雾气。有人用手指在上面画画，有人写字，更多的人只是看着那层雾慢慢凝成水珠，顺着玻璃滑下来。我观察过很多次，发现一个规律：独自来的人更容易注意到雾气，而结伴来的人几乎不会看窗户。这大概说明了什么。孤独的人对环境的感知更敏锐，因为他们没有对话来填满注意力。于是周围的一切——光线、温度、声音、雾气的形成与消散——都变成了他们的伴侣。今天我坐在角落，看着一个年轻女孩在雾气上画了一只猫，然后用手掌一擦，猫就消失了。她又画了一只，又擦掉。反复了五六次，每次画的猫都不一样。我忽然想，也许她不是在画猫，而是在练习告别。每一次擦掉都是一次小小的放手，每一次重画都是一次重新开始。雾气终究会散去，但她练习过的那种从容，也许会留在手指的肌肉记忆里。
text_pinyin: Dōngtiān de kāfēiguǎn lǐ, kào chuāng de zuòwèi zǒng shì zuì xiān bèi zhàn mǎn. Bú shì yīnwèi fēngjǐng hǎo——wàimiàn huīméngméng de, shù yě tū le——ér shì yīnwèi bōli shàng huì qǐ yī céng wùqì. Yǒu rén yòng shǒuzhǐ zài shàngmian huà huà, yǒu rén xiě zì, gèng duō de rén zhǐshì kàn zhe nà céng wù mànmàn níng chéng shuǐzhū, shùn zhe bōli huá xiàlái. Wǒ guānchá guò hěn duō cì, fāxiàn yī gè guīlǜ: dúzì lái de rén gèng róngyì zhùyì dào wùqì, ér jiébàn lái de rén jīhū bú huì kàn chuānghu. Zhè dàgài shuōmíng le shénme. Gūdú de rén duì huánjìng de gǎnzhī gèng mǐnruì, yīnwèi tāmen méiyǒu duìhuà lái tiánmǎn zhùyìlì. Yúshì zhōuwéi de yīqiè——guāngxiàn, wēndù, shēngyīn, wùqì de xíngchéng yǔ xiāosàn——dōu biànchéng le tāmen de bànlǚ. Jīntiān wǒ zuò zài jiǎoluò, kàn zhe yī gè niánqīng nǚhái zài wùqì shàng huà le yī zhī māo, ránhòu yòng shǒuzhǎng yī cā, māo jiù xiāoshī le. Tā yòu huà le yī zhī, yòu cā diào. Fǎnfù le wǔ liù cì, měi cì huà de māo dōu bù yīyàng. Wǒ hūrán xiǎng, yěxǔ tā bú shì zài huà māo, ér shì zài liànxí gàobié. Měi yī cì cā diào dōu shì yī cì xiǎo xiǎo de fàngshǒu, měi yī cì chóng huà dōu shì yī cì chóngxīn kāishǐ. Wùqì zhōngjiù huì sàn qù, dàn tā liànxí guò de nà zhǒng cóngróng, yěxǔ huì liú zài shǒuzhǐ de jīròu jìyì lǐ.
text_en: In winter cafés, the window seats are always claimed first. Not for the view—it's gray outside and the trees are bare—but because a layer of fog forms on the glass. Some trace drawings with their fingers, some write words, but most just watch the mist slowly bead into droplets that slide down the pane. I've observed this many times and noticed a pattern: people who come alone are more likely to notice the fog, while those who come with companions almost never look at the window. This probably says something. Solitary people perceive their surroundings more keenly, because they have no conversation to fill their attention. So everything around them—light, temperature, sound, the forming and fading of mist—becomes their companion. Today I sat in the corner watching a young woman draw a cat on the fogged glass, then wipe it away with her palm. She drew another, wiped it again. Five or six times, each cat different from the last. It suddenly occurred to me that perhaps she wasn't drawing cats—she was practicing goodbye. Each erasure was a small letting go, each redrawing a fresh start. The fog will eventually clear, but the composure she practiced may linger in the muscle memory of her fingers.

questions.q_zh: 作者发现了什么关于独自来的人和结伴来的人的区别？
questions.q_en: What distinction did the author observe between people who come alone and those who come with others?

options.text: 独自来的人更容易注意到玻璃上的雾气
options.pinyin: dúzì lái de rén gèng róngyì zhùyì dào bōli shàng de wùqì
options.text_en: People who come alone are more likely to notice the fog on the glass

options.text: 结伴来的人更喜欢靠窗的座位
options.pinyin: jiébàn lái de rén gèng xǐhuan kào chuāng de zuòwèi
options.text_en: People with companions prefer window seats

options.text: 独自来的人喝咖啡更慢
options.pinyin: dúzì lái de rén hē kāfēi gèng màn
options.text_en: People who come alone drink coffee more slowly

questions.q_zh: 作者认为女孩反复画猫又擦掉的行为象征着什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think the girl's repeated drawing and erasing symbolizes?

options.text: 她在练习画画技巧
options.pinyin: tā zài liànxí huà huà jìqiǎo
options.text_en: She was practicing drawing technique

options.text: 她在练习告别和重新开始
options.pinyin: tā zài liànxí gàobié hé chóngxīn kāishǐ
options.text_en: She was practicing farewell and starting over

options.text: 她很无聊，在打发时间
options.pinyin: tā hěn wúliáo, zài dǎfā shíjiān
options.text_en: She was bored, killing time

title: The Sound of Rain on Different Roofs
title_zh: 雨落在不同屋顶的声音
text_zh: 我在不同的城市住过很多种房子，发现雨打在不同材质的屋顶上，声音完全不一样。瓦片屋顶是清脆的，像有人在轻轻敲一排排小碗。铁皮屋顶是急促而密集的，像一万颗小石子同时落下来，带着金属的回响。混凝土屋顶几乎没有声音，雨水被吸收了，只有很大的雨才能听到沉闷的嗡嗡声。木头屋顶介于瓦片和混凝土之间，温和、低沉，像远处有人在打鼓。我最喜欢的是老家那种瓦片屋顶。小时候下雨的午后，我躺在床上听雨声，能分辨出雨的大小、方向甚至温度。冷雨打在瓦上更脆，暖雨更沉。风从东边来的时候，东边的瓦先响；风转了方向，声音的重心也跟着移动。这就像一种天然的环绕声系统，不需要任何设备，只需要一排老瓦片和一场恰到好处的雨。后来住进城市的高层公寓，我再也听不到雨声了。窗户是双层隔音的，空调的嗡嗡声代替了一切。有时候我会打开一个播放雨声的应用程序，但怎么听都觉得差了什么。差的大概不是声音本身，而是那种「此刻正在下雨，而我恰好在听」的即时感。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài bùtóng de chéngshì zhù guò hěn duō zhǒng fángzi, fāxiàn yǔ dǎ zài bùtóng cáizhì de wūdǐng shàng, shēngyīn wánquán bù yīyàng. Wǎpiàn wūdǐng shì qīngcuì de, xiàng yǒu rén zài qīngqīng qiāo yī pái pái xiǎo wǎn. Tiěpí wūdǐng shì jícù ér mìjí de, xiàng yī wàn kē xiǎo shízǐ tóngshí luò xiàlái, dài zhe jīnshǔ de huíxiǎng. Hùnníngtǔ wūdǐng jīhū méiyǒu shēngyīn, yǔshuǐ bèi xīshōu le, zhǐyǒu hěn dà de yǔ cái néng tīng dào chénmèn de wēngwēng shēng. Mùtou wūdǐng jiè yú wǎpiàn hé hùnníngtǔ zhī jiān, wēnhé, dīchén, xiàng yuǎnchù yǒu rén zài dǎ gǔ. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan de shì lǎojiā nà zhǒng wǎpiàn wūdǐng. Xiǎoshíhou xià yǔ de wǔhòu, wǒ tǎng zài chuáng shàng tīng yǔshēng, néng fēnbiàn chū yǔ de dàxiǎo, fāngxiàng shènzhì wēndù. Lěng yǔ dǎ zài wǎ shàng gèng cuì, nuǎn yǔ gèng chén. Fēng cóng dōngbiān lái de shíhou, dōngbiān de wǎ xiān xiǎng; fēng zhuǎn le fāngxiàng, shēngyīn de zhòngxīn yě gēn zhe yídòng. Zhè jiù xiàng yī zhǒng tiānrán de huánrào shēng xìtǒng, bù xūyào rènhé shèbèi, zhǐ xūyào yī pái lǎo wǎpiàn hé yī chǎng qiàdàohǎochù de yǔ. Hòulái zhù jìn chéngshì de gāocéng gōngyù, wǒ zài yě tīng bú dào yǔshēng le. Chuānghu shì shuāngcéng géyīn de, kōngtiáo de wēngwēng shēng dàitì le yīqiè. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì dǎkāi yī gè bōfàng yǔshēng de yìngyòng chéngxù, dàn zěnme tīng dōu juéde chà le shénme. Chà de dàgài bú shì shēngyīn běnshēn, ér shì nà zhǒng「cǐkè zhèngzài xià yǔ, ér wǒ qiàhǎo zài tīng」de jíshí gǎn.
text_en: I've lived in many kinds of houses across different cities and discovered that rain sounds completely different on different roofing materials. Tile roofs are crisp, like someone gently tapping rows of small bowls. Tin roofs are rapid and dense, like ten thousand pebbles falling at once with a metallic echo. Concrete roofs are nearly silent—the water is absorbed, and only heavy rain produces a muffled hum. Wooden roofs fall between tile and concrete: mild, low, like distant drumming. My favorite was the tile roof of my hometown home. On rainy afternoons as a child, I'd lie in bed listening to the rain and could distinguish its intensity, direction, even temperature. Cold rain on tiles sounds crisper; warm rain, deeper. When wind came from the east, the eastern tiles sounded first; when the wind shifted, the center of sound shifted with it. It was like a natural surround-sound system, requiring no equipment—just a row of old tiles and a well-timed rain. Later I moved into a high-rise apartment in the city and could no longer hear the rain. The windows are double-paned for insulation; the hum of air conditioning replaced everything. Sometimes I open a rain-sound app, but something always feels missing. What's missing probably isn't the sound itself, but the immediacy of 'it is raining right now, and I happen to be listening.'

questions.q_zh: 作者认为雨声应用程序缺少的是什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think the rain-sound app is missing?

options.text: 音质不够好
options.pinyin: yīnzhì bú gòu hǎo
options.text_en: The sound quality isn't good enough

options.text: 「此刻正在下雨而我恰好在听」的即时感
options.pinyin: 「cǐkè zhèngzài xià yǔ ér wǒ qiàhǎo zài tīng」de jíshí gǎn
options.text_en: The immediacy of 'it's raining right now and I happen to be listening'

options.text: 雨声的种类不够多
options.pinyin: yǔshēng de zhǒnglèi bú gòu duō
options.text_en: Not enough varieties of rain sounds

questions.q_zh: 根据文章，冷雨和暖雨打在瓦片上的声音有什么不同？
questions.q_en: According to the passage, how do cold rain and warm rain sound different on tiles?

options.text: 冷雨更脆，暖雨更沉
options.pinyin: lěng yǔ gèng cuì, nuǎn yǔ gèng chén
options.text_en: Cold rain is crisper, warm rain is deeper

options.text: 冷雨更大声，暖雨更安静
options.pinyin: lěng yǔ gèng dà shēng, nuǎn yǔ gèng ānjìng
options.text_en: Cold rain is louder, warm rain is quieter

options.text: 冷雨节奏更快，暖雨节奏更慢
options.pinyin: lěng yǔ jiézòu gèng kuài, nuǎn yǔ jiézòu gèng màn
options.text_en: Cold rain has faster rhythm, warm rain slower

title: The Last Customer
text_zh: 我常去的那家面馆每天晚上十点关门。但我发现，真正决定关门时间的不是时钟，而是最后一位客人。如果九点半还没人来，老板就开始收拾，擦桌子，把调料瓶一个个盖好，椅子倒扣在桌面上。但如果九点五十来了一个人，他会重新把灶台打开，像没有任何不耐烦地煮一碗面。我问过他为什么不严格按时间关门。他说：「人家大晚上的还要出来吃面，要么是加班太晚，要么是心里不太舒服，怎么都是需要一碗热面的人。我早关十分钟，对我来说只是早睡十分钟；但对那个人来说，可能就是在一个疲惫的夜晚被拒绝了一次。」这番话我记了很久。后来我注意到，很多那种在打烊前最后十分钟进来的客人，都不太说话。他们安静地坐下，安静地吃完，有时候抬头看看老板在忙什么，眼神里带着一种说不清的感激。不是那种需要表达的感激，是那种「你还在，真好」的感激。我想，这大概就是一间好店铺和一间普通店铺的区别。好的店铺卖的不只是食物，还有一种「来了就不会被拒绝」的承诺。
text_pinyin: Wǒ cháng qù de nà jiā miànguǎn měi tiān wǎnshang shí diǎn guānmén. Dàn wǒ fāxiàn, zhēnzhèng juédìng guānmén shíjiān de bú shì shízhōng, ér shì zuìhòu yī wèi kèrén. Rúguǒ jiǔ diǎn bàn hái méi rén lái, lǎobǎn jiù kāishǐ shōushi, cā zhuōzi, bǎ tiáoliào píng yī gè gè gài hǎo, yǐzi dào kòu zài zhuōmiàn shàng. Dàn rúguǒ jiǔ diǎn wǔshí lái le yī gè rén, tā huì chóngxīn bǎ zàotái dǎkāi, xiàng méiyǒu rènhé bù nàifán de zhǔ yī wǎn miàn. Wǒ wèn guò tā wèishéme bù yángé àn shíjiān guānmén. Tā shuō: 「Rénjia dà wǎnshang de hái yào chūlái chī miàn, yàome shì jiābān tài wǎn, yàome shì xīnlǐ bú tài shūfu, zěnme dōu shì xūyào yī wǎn rè miàn de rén. Wǒ zǎo guān shí fēnzhōng, duì wǒ lái shuō zhǐshì zǎo shuì shí fēnzhōng; dàn duì nàge rén lái shuō, kěnéng jiùshì zài yī gè píbèi de yèwǎn bèi jùjué le yī cì.」Zhè fān huà wǒ jì le hěn jiǔ. Hòulái wǒ zhùyì dào, hěn duō nà zhǒng zài dǎyáng qián zuìhòu shí fēnzhōng jìnlái de kèrén, dōu bú tài shuōhuà. Tāmen ānjìng de zuò xià, ānjìng de chī wán, yǒu shíhou tái tóu kàn kàn lǎobǎn zài máng shénme, yǎnshén lǐ dài zhe yī zhǒng shuō bù qīng de gǎnjī. Bú shì nà zhǒng xūyào biǎodá de gǎnjī, shì nà zhǒng「nǐ hái zài, zhēn hǎo」de gǎnjī. Wǒ xiǎng, zhè dàgài jiùshì yī jiān hǎo diànpù hé yī jiān pǔtōng diànpù de qūbié. Hǎo de diànpù mài de bù zhǐshì shíwù, hái yǒu yī zhǒng「lái le jiù bú huì bèi jùjué」de chéngnuò.
text_en: The noodle shop I frequent closes at ten every evening. But I've noticed that what truly determines closing time isn't the clock—it's the last customer. If no one comes by nine-thirty, the owner starts cleaning up: wiping tables, capping condiment bottles one by one, flipping chairs upside down on the tabletops. But if someone walks in at nine-fifty, he'll relight the stove and cook a bowl of noodles without a trace of impatience. I once asked him why he doesn't close strictly on time. He said: 'Someone coming out this late for noodles is either working overtime too late or feeling uneasy inside—either way, they're someone who needs a hot bowl of noodles. Closing ten minutes early only means I sleep ten minutes earlier; but for that person, it might mean being turned away on an exhausting night.' I remembered those words for a long time. Later I noticed that many customers who come in during the last ten minutes before closing barely speak. They sit down quietly, eat quietly, sometimes glancing up to see what the owner is doing, with a kind of ineffable gratitude in their eyes. Not the kind of gratitude that needs expressing, but the kind that says 'you're still here—that's good.' I think this is probably the difference between a good shop and an ordinary one. A good shop sells not just food, but a promise: 'come in, and you won't be turned away.'

questions.q_zh: 老板为什么不严格按时间关门？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the owner close strictly on time?

options.text: 他想多赚点钱
options.pinyin: tā xiǎng duō zhuàn diǎn qián
options.text_en: He wants to earn more money

options.text: 他觉得晚来的人更需要一碗热面，不想拒绝他们
options.pinyin: tā juéde wǎn lái de rén gèng xūyào yī wǎn rè miàn, bù xiǎng jùjué tāmen
options.text_en: He feels late customers need a hot bowl of noodles more and doesn't want to refuse them

options.text: 他自己也睡得很晚
options.pinyin: tā zìjǐ yě shuì de hěn wǎn
options.text_en: He stays up late himself anyway

questions.q_zh: 文章最后说「好的店铺卖的不只是食物」，还卖什么？
questions.q_en: The passage says a good shop sells more than food—what else does it sell?

options.text: 优质的服务态度
options.pinyin: yōuzhì de fúwù tàidu
options.text_en: Quality service attitude

options.text: 一种「来了就不会被拒绝」的承诺
options.pinyin: yī zhǒng「lái le jiù bú huì bèi jùjué」de chéngnuò
options.text_en: A promise that 'you won't be turned away once you come'

options.text: 温暖的用餐环境
options.pinyin: wēnnuǎn de yòngcān huánjìng
options.text_en: A warm dining environment

title: Shadows of Bicycles
text_zh: 下午三点左右的阳光最适合看影子。太阳不高不低，影子被拉长但不至于变形，每个细节都清晰可辨。我住的那条巷子里停着很多自行车，下午的时候，地上会出现一排影子，车轮、车把、车筐都投射在灰白色的水泥地上。我注意到一件有意思的事情：自行车的影子比自行车本身更好看。因为影子把三维的东西压缩成了二维，去掉了锈迹、划痕、掉漆这些岁月留下的痕迹，只保留了最纯粹的轮廓。一辆老旧的自行车，影子看起来跟新的一样。这让我想到一个关于记忆的问题。我们对一个人的记忆，是不是也像影子一样？时间会慢慢抹去细节——他说过的蠢话、他偶尔的自私、他让你失望的那些时刻——最终只留下一个干净的轮廓：他对你笑的样子，他递给你一杯水的动作，他离开时回头看了你一眼。也许遗忘不是一种损失，而是一种精炼。就像阳光只投射出物体的形状，遗忘只保留了关系的本质。那些自行车每天承受着风吹日晒，但它们的影子永远是新的。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ sān diǎn zuǒyòu de yángguāng zuì shìhé kàn yǐngzi. Tàiyáng bù gāo bù dī, yǐngzi bèi lā cháng dàn bú zhìyú biànxíng, měi gè xìjié dōu qīngxī kě biàn. Wǒ zhù de nà tiáo xiàngzi lǐ tíng zhe hěn duō zìxíngchē, xiàwǔ de shíhou, dì shàng huì chūxiàn yī pái yǐngzi, chēlún, chēbǎ, chēkuāng dōu tóushè zài huībái sè de shuǐní dì shàng. Wǒ zhùyì dào yī jiàn yǒu yìsi de shìqing: zìxíngchē de yǐngzi bǐ zìxíngchē běnshēn gèng hǎokàn. Yīnwèi yǐngzi bǎ sān wéi de dōngxi yāsuō chéng le èr wéi, qùdiào le xiùjì, huáhén, diào qī zhèxiē suìyuè liú xià de hénjì, zhǐ bǎoliú le zuì chúncuì de lúnkuò. Yī liàng lǎojiù de zìxíngchē, yǐngzi kàn qǐlái gēn xīn de yīyàng. Zhè ràng wǒ xiǎng dào yī gè guānyú jìyì de wèntí. Wǒmen duì yī gè rén de jìyì, shì bu shì yě xiàng yǐngzi yīyàng? Shíjiān huì mànmàn mǒ qù xìjié——tā shuō guò de chǔn huà, tā ǒu'ěr de zìsī, tā ràng nǐ shīwàng de nàxiē shíkè——zuìzhōng zhǐ liú xià yī gè gānjìng de lúnkuò: tā duì nǐ xiào de yàngzi, tā dì gěi nǐ yī bēi shuǐ de dòngzuò, tā líkāi shí huí tóu kàn le nǐ yī yǎn. Yěxǔ yíwàng bú shì yī zhǒng sǔnshī, ér shì yī zhǒng jīngliàn. Jiù xiàng yángguāng zhǐ tóushè chū wùtǐ de xíngzhuàng, yíwàng zhǐ bǎoliú le guānxi de běnzhì. Nàxiē zìxíngchē měi tiān chéngshòu zhe fēngchuī rìshài, dàn tāmen de yǐngzi yǒngyuǎn shì xīn de.
text_en: The sunlight around three in the afternoon is best for watching shadows. The sun sits neither too high nor too low; shadows are stretched but not distorted, every detail sharply defined. Many bicycles are parked along my alley, and in the afternoon a row of shadows appears on the ground—wheels, handlebars, baskets all projected onto the pale gray cement. I noticed something interesting: the shadows of bicycles look better than the bicycles themselves. Shadows compress three dimensions into two, stripping away rust, scratches, chipped paint—all the marks of passing years—leaving only the purest outline. An old, worn bicycle casts a shadow that looks brand new. This made me think about memory. Are our memories of a person like shadows too? Time gradually erases the details—the foolish things they said, their occasional selfishness, the moments they disappointed you—until only a clean silhouette remains: the way they smiled at you, the gesture of handing you a glass of water, the backward glance as they left. Perhaps forgetting isn't a loss but a refinement. Just as sunlight projects only an object's shape, forgetting preserves only the essence of a relationship. Those bicycles endure wind and sun every day, but their shadows are forever new.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么觉得自行车的影子比自行车本身更好看？
questions.q_en: Why does the author think bicycle shadows look better than the bicycles themselves?

options.text: 影子去掉了锈迹和划痕，只保留了最纯粹的轮廓
options.pinyin: yǐngzi qùdiào le xiùjì hé huáhén, zhǐ bǎoliú le zuì chúncuì de lúnkuò
options.text_en: Shadows remove rust and scratches, keeping only the purest outline

options.text: 影子的颜色更好看
options.pinyin: yǐngzi de yánsè gèng hǎokàn
options.text_en: The color of shadows is more attractive

options.text: 影子比自行车更大
options.pinyin: yǐngzi bǐ zìxíngchē gèng dà
options.text_en: Shadows are larger than bicycles

questions.q_zh: 作者认为遗忘和影子有什么共同点？
questions.q_en: What do the author and shadows have in common according to the passage?

options.text: 两者都是一种损失
options.pinyin: liǎng zhě dōu shì yī zhǒng sǔnshī
options.text_en: Both are a form of loss

options.text: 两者都是一种精炼——去掉细节，保留本质
options.pinyin: liǎng zhě dōu shì yī zhǒng jīngliàn——qùdiào xìjié, bǎoliú běnzhì
options.text_en: Both are a refinement—removing detail, preserving essence

options.text: 两者都会随时间变化
options.pinyin: liǎng zhě dōu huì suí shíjiān biànhuà
options.text_en: Both change with time

title: The Thermometer Outside the Window
text_zh: 楼下王阿姨的窗台上挂着一个老式温度计，红色酒精柱的那种。每天早上她都要看一眼，然后决定今天穿什么。手机上也能看气温，但她不信。她说：「手机上的温度是气象局测的，离我家好几公里远。我窗台上的温度才是我真正要面对的温度。」我一开始觉得这话没什么道理，后来想想，其实很有道理。气象数据是一种平均值，一种抽象。它告诉你这个城市的温度，但不是你阳台上的温度，不是你出门拐弯处那阵穿堂风的温度，不是你走到地铁站那段上坡路上因为运动而感受到的温度。真正的体感温度是非常个人的，跟你的体质、心情、睡眠质量、早餐吃了什么都有关系。王阿姨的温度计虽然可能不如气象站的精确，但它测的是「她的温度」——她所站的那个位置、她的朝向、她的楼层高度。这是一种拒绝被数据代表的坚持。我们的生活越来越被各种平均值包围：平均房价、平均薪资、平均寿命。但没有人活在平均值里。每个人都活在自己窗台上那个温度计的读数里。
text_pinyin: Lóu xià Wáng āyí de chuāngtái shàng guà zhe yī gè lǎoshì wēndùjì, hóngsè jiǔjīng zhù de nà zhǒng. Měi tiān zǎoshang tā dōu yào kàn yī yǎn, ránhòu juédìng jīntiān chuān shénme. Shǒujī shàng yě néng kàn qìwēn, dàn tā bú xìn. Tā shuō: 「Shǒujī shàng de wēndù shì qìxiàngjú cè de, lí wǒ jiā hǎo jǐ gōnglǐ yuǎn. Wǒ chuāngtái shàng de wēndù cái shì wǒ zhēnzhèng yào miànduì de wēndù.」Wǒ yī kāishǐ juéde zhè huà méi shénme dàolǐ, hòulái xiǎng xiǎng, qíshí hěn yǒu dàolǐ. Qìxiàng shùjù shì yī zhǒng píngjūn zhí, yī zhǒng chōuxiàng. Tā gàosu nǐ zhège chéngshì de wēndù, dàn bú shì nǐ yángtái shàng de wēndù, bú shì nǐ chūmén guǎi wān chù nà zhèn chuāntáng fēng de wēndù, bú shì nǐ zǒu dào dìtiě zhàn nà duàn shàng pō lù shàng yīnwèi yùndòng ér gǎnshòu dào de wēndù. Zhēnzhèng de tǐgǎn wēndù shì fēicháng gèrén de, gēn nǐ de tǐzhì, xīnqíng, shuìmián zhìliàng, zǎocān chī le shénme dōu yǒu guānxi. Wáng āyí de wēndùjì suīrán kěnéng bùrú qìxiàng zhàn de jīngquè, dàn tā cè de shì「tā de wēndù」——tā suǒ zhàn de nàge wèizhì, tā de cháoxiàng, tā de lóucéng gāodù. Zhè shì yī zhǒng jùjué bèi shùjù dàibiǎo de jiānchí. Wǒmen de shēnghuó yuè lái yuè bèi gè zhǒng píngjūn zhí bāowéi: píngjūn fángjià, píngjūn xīnzī, píngjūn shòumìng. Dàn méiyǒu rén huó zài píngjūn zhí lǐ. Měi gè rén dōu huó zài zìjǐ chuāngtái shàng nàge wēndùjì de dúshù lǐ.
text_en: Auntie Wang downstairs has an old-fashioned thermometer on her windowsill—the kind with a red alcohol column. Every morning she checks it, then decides what to wear. She could check the temperature on her phone, but she doesn't trust it. She says: 'The phone temperature is measured by the weather bureau, several kilometers from my home. The temperature on my windowsill is the temperature I actually have to face.' At first I thought this made no sense, but thinking more, it makes a lot of sense. Meteorological data is an average, an abstraction. It tells you the city's temperature, but not your balcony's temperature, not the temperature of that cross-draft at the corner when you step out, not the temperature you feel walking uphill to the subway station because of exertion. True perceived temperature is deeply personal—related to your constitution, mood, sleep quality, what you ate for breakfast. Auntie Wang's thermometer may not be as precise as the weather station's, but it measures 'her temperature'—the position where she stands, her building's orientation, her floor height. This is a kind of insistence on not being represented by data. Our lives are increasingly surrounded by averages: average housing prices, average salaries, average lifespans. But nobody lives inside an average. Everyone lives in the reading on the thermometer on their own windowsill.

questions.q_zh: 王阿姨为什么不相信手机上的气温？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't Auntie Wang trust the temperature on her phone?

options.text: 她不会用手机
options.pinyin: tā bú huì yòng shǒujī
options.text_en: She doesn't know how to use a phone

options.text: 手机上的温度是远处气象局测的，不是她窗台的真实温度
options.pinyin: shǒujī shàng de wēndù shì yuǎnchù qìxiàngjú cè de, bú shì tā chuāngtái de zhēnshí wēndù
options.text_en: The phone temperature is measured far away, not the real temperature at her windowsill

options.text: 她觉得手机温度经常出错
options.pinyin: tā juéde shǒujī wēndù jīngcháng chū cuò
options.text_en: She thinks phone temperatures are often wrong

questions.q_zh: 文章最后的核心观点是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the core idea at the end of the passage?

options.text: 气象数据不准确，不值得信任
options.pinyin: qìxiàng shùjù bù zhǔnquè, bù zhíde xìnrèn
options.text_en: Weather data is inaccurate and untrustworthy

options.text: 没有人真正活在平均值里，每个人的体验都是个人的
options.pinyin: méiyǒu rén zhēnzhèng huó zài píngjūn zhí lǐ, měi gè rén de tǐyàn dōu shì gèrén de
options.text_en: Nobody truly lives in averages—everyone's experience is personal

options.text: 老式温度计比电子设备更可靠
options.pinyin: lǎoshì wēndùjì bǐ diànzǐ shèbèi gèng kěkào
options.text_en: Old-fashioned thermometers are more reliable than electronic devices

title: The Way Old People Sit
text_zh: 公园里有一排长椅，每天下午都坐满了老人。我路过的时候仔细看过，发现每个人坐着的姿势都不一样，而且这些姿势似乎透露着各自不同的人生态度。有的老人坐得很直，双手放在膝盖上，像是随时准备站起来去做什么事。这种人大概一辈子都很忙碌，即使退休了也闲不下来。有的老人身体往后靠，双腿伸得很长，脸朝着太阳，眯着眼睛，像一只晒太阳的猫。这种人应该是终于学会了什么都不做的艺术。还有一种我特别喜欢观察的：微微前倾，两只手交叉放在拐杖顶端，下巴搁在手背上，眼睛半闭半睁地看着前方。这种姿势兼具了放松和警觉，好像在说：「我不需要再去做什么了，但我还在看着这个世界。」他们看的不是具体的什么——不是走过的行人，不是飞过的鸟，不是远处的楼——他们看的是时间本身在流动。那种目光是超越了具体对象的，它穿过公园，穿过树木，落在某个不属于任何空间的地方。年轻人是做不到这种目光的，因为年轻人的眼睛总是在寻找什么。只有活够了、看够了的人，才能学会不寻找地看。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yī pái chángyǐ, měi tiān xiàwǔ dōu zuò mǎn le lǎorén. Wǒ lùguò de shíhou zǐxì kàn guò, fāxiàn měi gè rén zuò zhe de zīshì dōu bù yīyàng, érqiě zhèxiē zīshì sìhū tòulù zhe gèzì bùtóng de rénshēng tàidu. Yǒu de lǎorén zuò de hěn zhí, shuāng shǒu fàng zài xīgài shàng, xiàng shì suíshí zhǔnbèi zhàn qǐlái qù zuò shénme shì. Zhè zhǒng rén dàgài yī bèizi dōu hěn mánglù, jíshǐ tuìxiū le yě xián bú xiàlái. Yǒu de lǎorén shēntǐ wǎng hòu kào, shuāng tuǐ shēn de hěn cháng, liǎn cháo zhe tàiyáng, mī zhe yǎnjīng, xiàng yī zhī shài tàiyáng de māo. Zhè zhǒng rén yīnggāi shì zhōngyú xuéhuì le shénme dōu bú zuò de yìshù. Hái yǒu yī zhǒng wǒ tèbié xǐhuan guānchá de: wēiwēi qián qīng, liǎng zhī shǒu jiāochā fàng zài guǎizhàng dǐngduān, xiàba gē zài shǒubèi shàng, yǎnjīng bàn bì bàn zhēng de kàn zhe qiánfāng. Zhè zhǒng zīshì jiānjù le fàngsōng hé jǐngjué, hǎoxiàng zài shuō: 「Wǒ bù xūyào zài qù zuò shénme le, dàn wǒ hái zài kàn zhe zhège shìjiè.」Tāmen kàn de bú shì jùtǐ de shénme——bú shì zǒu guò de xíngrén, bú shì fēi guò de niǎo, bú shì yuǎnchù de lóu——tāmen kàn de shì shíjiān běnshēn zài liúdòng. Nà zhǒng mùguāng shì chāoyuè le jùtǐ duìxiàng de, tā chuān guò gōngyuán, chuān guò shùmù, luò zài mǒu gè bù shǔyú rènhé kōngjiān de dìfāng. Niánqīng rén shì zuò bú dào zhè zhǒng mùguāng de, yīnwèi niánqīng rén de yǎnjīng zǒng shì zài xúnzhǎo shénme. Zhǐyǒu huó gòu le, kàn gòu le de rén, cái néng xuéhuì bù xúnzhǎo de kàn.
text_en: There's a row of benches in the park, filled with elderly people every afternoon. Passing by and looking carefully, I noticed everyone sits differently, and these postures seem to reveal distinct life philosophies. Some sit bolt upright, hands on knees, as if ready to stand and do something at any moment. These people were probably busy their whole lives—even retired, they can't sit still. Others lean back, legs stretched long, faces toward the sun, eyes squinting like a cat basking in warmth. They've probably finally mastered the art of doing nothing. Then there's one posture I especially like to observe: leaning slightly forward, hands crossed atop a cane, chin resting on the back of the hands, eyes half-closed gazing ahead. This posture blends relaxation with alertness, as if saying: 'I don't need to do anything anymore, but I'm still watching the world.' They're not watching anything specific—not the passersby, not the birds overhead, not the buildings in the distance—they're watching time itself flow. That gaze transcends particular objects; it passes through the park, through the trees, and lands somewhere that belongs to no space at all. Young people can't achieve this kind of gaze, because young eyes are always searching for something. Only those who've lived enough and seen enough can learn to look without searching.

questions.q_zh: 作者最喜欢观察的坐姿有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What characterizes the sitting posture the author most enjoys observing?

options.text: 坐得很直，双手放在膝盖上
options.pinyin: zuò de hěn zhí, shuāng shǒu fàng zài xīgài shàng
options.text_en: Sitting very straight with hands on knees

options.text: 微微前倾，手搁在拐杖上，眼睛半闭半睁地看前方
options.pinyin: wēiwēi qián qīng, shǒu gē zài guǎizhàng shàng, yǎnjīng bàn bì bàn zhēng de kàn qiánfāng
options.text_en: Leaning slightly forward, hands on cane, eyes half-closed gazing ahead

options.text: 身体往后靠，双腿伸得很长
options.pinyin: shēntǐ wǎng hòu kào, shuāng tuǐ shēn de hěn cháng
options.text_en: Leaning back with legs stretched out long

questions.q_zh: 作者认为年轻人为什么做不到那种目光？
questions.q_en: Why does the author think young people can't achieve that kind of gaze?

options.text: 年轻人视力太好，看得太清楚
options.pinyin: niánqīng rén shìlì tài hǎo, kàn de tài qīngchu
options.text_en: Young people's vision is too good, they see too clearly

options.text: 年轻人的眼睛总是在寻找什么，还没学会不寻找地看
options.pinyin: niánqīng rén de yǎnjīng zǒng shì zài xúnzhǎo shénme, hái méi xuéhuì bù xúnzhǎo de kàn
options.text_en: Young eyes are always searching; they haven't learned to look without searching

options.text: 年轻人没有耐心坐在公园里
options.pinyin: niánqīng rén méiyǒu nàixīn zuò zài gōngyuán lǐ
options.text_en: Young people lack patience to sit in the park

title: The Smell of an Old Bookstore
text_zh: 离我家不远有一间旧书店，走进去的第一秒，你会被一种气味包裹住。那是纸张、灰尘、木头书架和微弱的霉气混合在一起的味道。很多人会觉得不好闻，但我觉得那是一种非常诚实的气味。它在告诉你：这里的东西已经被时间处理过了。新书店是没有气味的，或者说，它的气味是被设计过的——咖啡、香薰、新印的油墨。这些气味都在说「请留下来」，是一种商业策略。旧书店的气味不取悦你，它只是存在。你爱来就来，不来也无所谓。这种不刻意讨好的态度反而让人放松。我在那里翻过一本1987年出版的散文集，书页已经泛黄，边缘有些脆了。翻开某一页，发现有人用铅笔在一句话下面画了一条线：「所有的相遇都是重逢。」我不知道画线的人是谁，也不知道他在什么样的心境下觉得这句话值得标注。但我知道，我此刻翻到这一页，看到这条线，构成了一种跨越时间的对话。他在三十多年前读到这句话的时候停了下来，我在今天读到它的时候也停了下来。我们被同一句话打动了，虽然隔着几十年。旧书店里最珍贵的不是那些书，而是书上留下的这些痕迹——它们是匿名的人际关系。
text_pinyin: Lí wǒ jiā bù yuǎn yǒu yī jiān jiù shūdiàn, zǒu jìnqù de dì yī miǎo, nǐ huì bèi yī zhǒng qìwèi bāoguǒ zhù. Nà shì zhǐzhāng, huīchén, mùtou shūjià hé wēiruò de méi qì hùnhé zài yīqǐ de wèidào. Hěn duō rén huì juéde bù hǎo wén, dàn wǒ juéde nà shì yī zhǒng fēicháng chéngshí de qìwèi. Tā zài gàosu nǐ: zhèlǐ de dōngxi yǐjīng bèi shíjiān chǔlǐ guò le. Xīn shūdiàn shì méiyǒu qìwèi de, huòzhě shuō, tā de qìwèi shì bèi shèjì guò de——kāfēi, xiāngxūn, xīn yìn de yóumò. Zhèxiē qìwèi dōu zài shuō「qǐng liú xiàlái」, shì yī zhǒng shāngyè cèlüè. Jiù shūdiàn de qìwèi bù qǔyuè nǐ, tā zhǐshì cúnzài. Nǐ ài lái jiù lái, bù lái yě wúsuǒwèi. Zhè zhǒng bú kèyì tǎohǎo de tàidu fǎn'ér ràng rén fàngsōng. Wǒ zài nàlǐ fān guò yī běn yī jiǔ bā qī nián chūbǎn de sǎnwénjí, shūyè yǐjīng fàn huáng, biānyuán yǒuxiē cuì le. Fānkāi mǒu yī yè, fāxiàn yǒu rén yòng qiānbǐ zài yī jù huà xiàmian huà le yī tiáo xiàn: 「Suǒyǒu de xiāngyù dōu shì chóngféng.」Wǒ bù zhīdào huà xiàn de rén shì shéi, yě bù zhīdào tā zài shénme yàng de xīnjìng xià juéde zhè jù huà zhíde biāozhù. Dàn wǒ zhīdào, wǒ cǐkè fān dào zhè yī yè, kàn dào zhè tiáo xiàn, gòuchéng le yī zhǒng kuàyuè shíjiān de duìhuà. Tā zài sānshí duō nián qián dú dào zhè jù huà de shíhou tíng le xiàlái, wǒ zài jīntiān dú dào tā de shíhou yě tíng le xiàlái. Wǒmen bèi tóng yī jù huà dǎdòng le, suīrán gé zhe jǐ shí nián. Jiù shūdiàn lǐ zuì zhēnguì de bú shì nàxiē shū, ér shì shū shàng liú xià de zhèxiē hénjì——tāmen shì nìmíng de rénjì guānxi.
text_en: Not far from my home is an old bookstore. The first second you step in, a smell envelops you. It's a blend of paper, dust, wooden shelves, and faint mildew. Many find it unpleasant, but I think it's a very honest scent. It's telling you: the things here have been processed by time. New bookstores are odorless—or rather, their scent is designed: coffee, aromatherapy, fresh ink. These smells all say 'please stay,' a commercial strategy. The old bookstore's smell doesn't try to please you; it simply exists. Come if you like, or don't—it makes no difference. This attitude of not deliberately trying to charm you is actually relaxing. I once leafed through an essay collection published in 1987, its pages yellowed and edges grown brittle. On one page, someone had drawn a pencil line under a sentence: 'All encounters are reunions.' I don't know who drew that line or what state of mind made them feel the sentence was worth marking. But I know that my turning to this page now and seeing that line creates a conversation across time. They stopped at this sentence over thirty years ago; I stopped at it today. We were both moved by the same words, though separated by decades. The most precious things in an old bookstore aren't the books themselves, but these traces left on them—they are anonymous human relationships.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么觉得旧书店的气味是「诚实的」？
questions.q_en: Why does the author consider the old bookstore's smell 'honest'?

options.text: 因为那种气味没有经过人工设计，是时间自然留下的
options.pinyin: yīnwèi nà zhǒng qìwèi méiyǒu jīngguò réngōng shèjì, shì shíjiān zìrán liú xià de
options.text_en: Because the smell isn't artificially designed—it's naturally left by time

options.text: 因为旧书的内容更真实
options.pinyin: yīnwèi jiù shū de nèiróng gèng zhēnshí
options.text_en: Because old books have more truthful content

options.text: 因为老板没有用香薰掩盖气味
options.pinyin: yīnwèi lǎobǎn méiyǒu yòng xiāngxūn yǎngài qìwèi
options.text_en: Because the owner doesn't mask the smell with aromatherapy

questions.q_zh: 作者在旧书上看到画线的句子后，有什么感悟？
questions.q_en: What insight did the author have after seeing the underlined sentence?

options.text: 他和几十年前画线的人形成了一种跨越时间的匿名对话
options.pinyin: tā hé jǐ shí nián qián huà xiàn de rén xíngchéng le yī zhǒng kuàyuè shíjiān de nìmíng duìhuà
options.text_en: He and the person who drew the line decades ago formed an anonymous conversation across time

options.text: 那句话的内容改变了他的人生观
options.pinyin: nà jù huà de nèiróng gǎibiàn le tā de rénshēngguān
options.text_en: The content of that sentence changed his life philosophy

options.text: 他想找到画线的那个人
options.pinyin: tā xiǎng zhǎo dào huà xiàn de nàge rén
options.text_en: He wanted to find the person who drew the line

title: Learning to Wait
text_zh: 我以前不会等待。排队的时候焦虑，等人的时候烦躁，等结果的时候反复刷新手机。后来我观察了一些很会等待的人，发现等待其实是一种技术。我的邻居老赵是一个退休的木匠。他做了一辈子家具，退休后唯一的爱好是钓鱼。有一次我看他钓了一下午，一条都没钓到，但他完全没有不耐烦的样子。我问他怎么做到的。他说：「做木匠的时候，刷了漆要等它干，胶了榫头要等它固，急是没有用的。等的时候你可以听听水的声音，看看天的颜色，想想晚上吃什么。等待不是空白，等待里面装的是生活本身。」这番话改变了我对等待的理解。我开始把等待当作一种被迫获得的自由时间——不用工作、不用回复消息、不用做任何决定，只需要待在原地。我开始在等红灯的时候观察路口的细节，在等电梯的时候听走廊里的回声，在等朋友迟到的时候欣赏咖啡馆里其他人的表情。渐渐地我发现，会等待的人不是因为他们更有耐心，而是因为他们更擅长往空白里填充内容。等待的质量，取决于你的感知力。一个感知力强的人，在任何地方都不会觉得无聊。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǐqián bú huì děngdài. Páiduì de shíhou jiāolǜ, děng rén de shíhou fánzào, děng jiéguǒ de shíhou fǎnfù shuāxīn shǒujī. Hòulái wǒ guānchá le yīxiē hěn huì děngdài de rén, fāxiàn děngdài qíshí shì yī zhǒng jìshù. Wǒ de línjū lǎo Zhào shì yī gè tuìxiū de mùjiàng. Tā zuò le yī bèizi jiājù, tuìxiū hòu wéiyī de àihào shì diàoyú. Yǒu yī cì wǒ kàn tā diào le yī xiàwǔ, yī tiáo dōu méi diào dào, dàn tā wánquán méiyǒu bù nàifán de yàngzi. Wǒ wèn tā zěnme zuò dào de. Tā shuō: 「Zuò mùjiàng de shíhou, shuā le qī yào děng tā gān, jiāo le sǔntóu yào děng tā gù, jí shì méiyǒu yòng de. Děng de shíhou nǐ kěyǐ tīng tīng shuǐ de shēngyīn, kàn kàn tiān de yánsè, xiǎng xiǎng wǎnshang chī shénme. Děngdài bú shì kòngbái, děngdài lǐmiàn zhuāng de shì shēnghuó běnshēn.」Zhè fān huà gǎibiàn le wǒ duì děngdài de lǐjiě. Wǒ kāishǐ bǎ děngdài dāng zuò yī zhǒng bèipò huòdé de zìyóu shíjiān——búyòng gōngzuò, búyòng huífù xiāoxi, búyòng zuò rènhé juédìng, zhǐ xūyào dāi zài yuándì. Wǒ kāishǐ zài děng hóngdēng de shíhou guānchá lùkǒu de xìjié, zài děng diàntī de shíhou tīng zǒuláng lǐ de huíshēng, zài děng péngyou chídào de shíhou xīnshǎng kāfēiguǎn lǐ qítā rén de biǎoqíng. Jiànjiàn de wǒ fāxiàn, huì děngdài de rén bú shì yīnwèi tāmen gèng yǒu nàixīn, ér shì yīnwèi tāmen gèng shàncháng wǎng kòngbái lǐ tiánchōng nèiróng. Děngdài de zhìliàng, qǔjué yú nǐ de gǎnzhī lì. Yī gè gǎnzhī lì qiáng de rén, zài rènhé dìfāng dōu bú huì juéde wúliáo.
text_en: I used to be bad at waiting. Anxious in queues, restless waiting for people, compulsively refreshing my phone while waiting for results. Then I observed some people who are good at waiting and discovered that waiting is actually a skill. My neighbor Old Zhao is a retired carpenter. He made furniture his whole life; after retirement, his only hobby is fishing. Once I watched him fish all afternoon without a single catch, yet he showed no impatience whatsoever. I asked him how. He said: 'When I was a carpenter, after applying varnish you had to wait for it to dry, after gluing a mortise you had to wait for it to set—rushing was useless. While waiting, you can listen to the sound of the water, look at the color of the sky, think about what to eat for dinner. Waiting isn't emptiness—waiting is filled with life itself.' Those words changed my understanding of waiting. I began treating it as a kind of involuntarily acquired free time—no need to work, reply to messages, or make any decisions; just stay in place. I started observing intersection details while waiting for red lights, listening to corridor echoes while waiting for elevators, appreciating other people's expressions in cafés while waiting for tardy friends. Gradually I realized that people who are good at waiting aren't more patient—they're better at filling emptiness with content. The quality of waiting depends on your perceptiveness. A person with strong perception never feels bored anywhere.

questions.q_zh: 老赵为什么钓不到鱼也不着急？
questions.q_en: Why wasn't Old Zhao anxious despite catching no fish?

options.text: 他已经习惯钓不到鱼了
options.pinyin: tā yǐjīng xíguàn diào bú dào yú le
options.text_en: He was used to not catching fish

options.text: 做木匠的经验教会了他等待不是空白，而是装满生活的时间
options.pinyin: zuò mùjiàng de jīngyàn jiāohuì le tā děngdài bú shì kòngbái, ér shì zhuāng mǎn shēnghuó de shíjiān
options.text_en: His carpentry experience taught him that waiting isn't emptiness but time filled with life

options.text: 他只是为了享受户外的空气
options.pinyin: tā zhǐshì wèile xiǎngshòu hùwài de kōngqì
options.text_en: He just wanted to enjoy the outdoor air

questions.q_zh: 作者认为等待的质量取决于什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author think determines the quality of waiting?

options.text: 等待的时间长短
options.pinyin: děngdài de shíjiān chángduǎn
options.text_en: The length of the wait

options.text: 一个人的感知力
options.pinyin: yī gè rén de gǎnzhī lì
options.text_en: A person's perceptiveness

options.text: 等待的原因是否值得
options.pinyin: děngdài de yuányīn shìfǒu zhíde
options.text_en: Whether the reason for waiting is worthwhile
